sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the Level 1 board state:
- Blue squares at cols 60-63 (rows 24-27 and 32-35)
- Charcoal object at cols 46-49 (rows 44-49)
- Yellow segment at cols 38-39 on the top black bar (rows 28-31)
- Yellow bar at cols 50-51 (rows 44-49)
- Target was at (33, 61), which is between the two blue squares, on the right side
In Level 1, the target (33, 61) is:
- Row 33 is between the two blue squares (rows 24-27 and 32-35)
- Col 61 is within the blue squares' column range (60-63)
So the target in Level 1 was at the position of the blue squares on the right side.
In Level 2, the blue squares are at cols 0-3 (rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47). The green segment is at cols 28-29 on the bottom black bar.
Following the Level 1 pattern, the target in Level 2 might be at the position of the blue squares, but adjusted for the level's layout.
Wait, in Level 1, the blue squares were at cols 60-63, and the target was at (33, 61). In Level 2, the blue squares are at cols 0-3. Following the pattern, the target might be at (33, 1) or (33, 2), which is within the blue squares' column range.
But I already tried clicking on (33, 28) and (33, 29), which are not within the blue squares' column range. Let me try clicking on (33, 1) or (33, 2).
Actually, wait. In Level 1, the target was at (33, 61), which is on row 33. Row 33 is between the two blue squares (rows 24-27 and 32-35). In Level 2, the blue squares are at rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47. Row 33 is between rows 24-27 and 36-39.
So the target in Level 2 might be at (33, 1) or (33, 2), which is on row 33 (between the blue squares) and within the blue squares' column range (0-3).
Let me try clicking on (33, 1) or (33, 2).

<parameter=code>

# In Level 2, blue squares are at cols 0-3
# Following the pattern, target might be at (33, 1) or (33, 2)

# Try (33, 1)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 1}])
pink = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')[0].count('P')
print(f"After (33,1): pink={pink}, level={current_frame.level}, completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

# Try (33, 2)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 2}])
pink = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')[0].count('P')
print(f"After (33,2): pink={pink}, level={current_frame.level}, completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
